mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 4.5 percent signals labor market tightness that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.5 percent within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal savings and new housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and consumer balance sheet health, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects improved supply chain throughput and capacity utilization, reducing input cost pressures and supporting profit margins, thereby generating positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO increases consistently precede negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds a 2.5 percent annualized threshold, it confirms economic momentum that overrides valuation concerns, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent annualized
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The concurrent rise in total nonfarm payrolls and the unemployment rate indicates a rapidly expanding labor force that sustains wage growth and consumption, driving positive equity valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE expansion signal fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of walk-forward periods.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in the consumer price index compresses real disposable income and forces monetary policy tightening, which mechanically reduces equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases do not precede negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the consumer price index by more than 2 percentage points, real borrowing costs become restrictive enough to contract corporate investment and equity multiples, producing negative forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The real rate threshold signal predicts negative 20-day returns in fewer than 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
